input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Run analyser as Batch job with per-account parallelisation

The analyser was a single Lambda processing all accounts serially,
which risks hitting the 15-minute timeout as more controls are added.
Now it runs as an AWS Batch job (same Docker image as the scanner)
with one job per account.

Changes:
- AnalysisEvent now accepts optional accountID to scope to one account
- processAnalysisEvent filters to that account when provided
- Analyser entry point supports both Lambda handler and Batch mode
  (reads EVENT from env var, same pattern as the scanner)
- Dockerfile uses ENTRY env var to select scanner or analyser entry
  point (defaults to scanner for backward compatibility)

The mcp-scanner trigger_analysis tool now submits Batch jobs directly
via SubmitJobCommand instead of invoking the Lambda. It takes an
accountIDs array and submits one job per account, using the crScanner
job definition with ENTRY=analyser override.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

cd /Users/adrian/IdeaProjects/Soteria/mcp-scanner && git add -A && git status --short && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Switch trigger_analysis from Lambda to Batch with per-account jobs

Replaces the single Lambda invocation with SubmitJobCommand to the
crBatch queue, one job per accountID. Uses the crScanner job definition
with ENTRY=analyser container override to run the analyser entry point
in the same Docker image.

Now requires accountIDs parameter so each account runs as a separate
Batch job with no timeout constraints, enabling parallel analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3
cd /Users/adrian/IdeaProjects/Soteria/cloudRisk && git push origin main 2>&1 && echo "---" && git push origin main 2>&1

ls src/shared/services/analyser/controls/s3/s3_35409.mjs src/shared/services/analyser/controls/s3/s3_41639.mjs src/shared/services/analyser/controls/s3/s3_65821.mjs src/shared/services/analyser/controls/s3/s3_96186.mjs 2>/dev/null && echo "---remaining S3---"; echo "===other services==="; for f in src/shared/services/analyser/controls/*.mjs; do echo "$f"; done | head -20

files=$(find src/shared/services/analyser/controls -name "*.mjs" \( -path "*/ecs/*" -o -path "*/ec2/*" -o -path "*/elasticache/*" -o -path "*/elasticbeanstalk/*" -o -path "*/elb/*" -o -path "*/emr/*" -o -path "*/eventbridge/*" -o -path "*/glue/*" -o -path "*/inspector/*" -o -path "*/macie/*" -o -path "*/mq/*" -o -path "*/msk/*" -o -path "*/pca/*" -o -path "*/rds/*" -o -path "*/redshift-serverless/*" -o -path "*/sagemaker/*" -o -path "*/route53/*" \) ! -name "index*" ! -name "base-control*")
echo "Ternary pattern:"; echo "$files" | xargs grep -l "result.status === 'PASS' ? passing++ : failing++" | wc -l
echo "If/else pattern:"; echo "$files" | xargs grep -l "if (result.status === 'PASS')" | wc -l
echo "Uses saveResult:"; echo "$files" | xargs grep -l "saveResult" | wc -l
echo "Imports saveResult:"; echo "$files" | xargs grep -l "import.*saveResult" | wc -l

input.command:
files_nontermary=$(find src/shared/services/analyser/controls -name "*.mjs" \( -path "*/ecs/*" -o -path "*/ec2/*" -o -path "*/elasticache/*" -o -path "*/elasticbeanstalk/*" -o -path "*/elb/*" -o -path "*/emr/*" -o -path "*/eventbridge/*" -o -path "*/glue/*" -o -path "*/inspector/*" -o -path "*/macie/*" -o -path "*/mq/*" -o -path "*/msk/*" -o -path "*/pca/*" -o -path "*/rds/*" -o -path "*/redshift-serverless/*" -o -path "*/sagemaker/*" -o -path "*/route53/*" \) ! -name "index*" ! -name "base-control*")

input.command:
# Check which parent .mjs files still have unmigrated pass/fail counters
migrated_parents="s3.mjs codebuild.mjs autoscaling.mjs dms.mjs datafirehose.mjs documentdb.mjs connect.mjs backup.mjs certificate.mjs datasync.mjs athena.mjs access-analyzer.mjs ami.mjs dns-firewall.mjs config-recorder.mjs ecr.mjs guardduty.mjs lambda.mjs network-firewall.mjs neptune.mjs kms.mjs securityhub.mjs ssm-patch.mjs transit-gateway.mjs vpc-endpoints.mjs sns.mjs sqs.mjs transfer.mjs waf.mjs vpc.mjs"
for f in src/shared/services/analyser/controls/*.mjs; do
  b=$(basename "$f")
  echo "$migrated_parents" | grep -qw "$b" && continue
  has_persist=$(grep -c "persistResourceFinding" "$f" 2>/dev/null)
  has_counters=$(grep -c "passing++\|failing++\|good++\|bad++" "$f" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$b persist=$has_persist counters=$has_counters"
done

git add -A && git status --short | wc -l && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Migrate 136 sub-control files to persistResourceFinding

Scripted migration of all sub-control .mjs files across 17 service
directories: ec2 (16), ecs (11), elasticache (7), elasticbeanstalk (3),
elb (13), emr (4), eventbridge (2), glue (2), inspector (4), macie (2),
mq (2), msk (5), pca (1), rds (31), redshift-serverless (7),
sagemaker (8), route53 (1).

Three patterns handled:
- Ternary pass/fail (33 files): persist after the ternary
- Class passingCount/failingCount (64 files): persist before saveResult
- Filter-based RDS/route53 (32 files): persist loop over collection
  before aggregate saveResult

Remaining: apigw.mjs, cloudTrail.mjs, config.mjs have inline legacy
controls with per-resource loops (not sub-control files).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add VPC Peering and EBS Snapshot analyser controls

New controls:
- soteria_47291 (HIGH) — VPC peering connections should only peer to
  accounts within the organisation. Checks requester/accepter OwnerId
  against the set of known accounts.
- soteria_83156 (MEDIUM) — VPC peering connections should be in active
  state. Flags pending/expired/rejected/failed connections.
- soteria_72418 (HIGH) — EBS snapshots should be encrypted at rest.
- soteria_59834 (CRITICAL) — EBS snapshots should not be publicly
  restorable (restorableByUserIds must not contain 'all').

Also maps existing RDS snapshot controls (soteria_71722, soteria_30594)
to the new 'rds-snapshot' section in the 'recover' category so they
appear under Recover in the security overview scoring.

Wired into processAnalysisEvent via runVPCPeeringControls and
runEBSSnapshotControls. All controls use persistResourceFinding for
the finding-transitions pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Run analyser as Batch job with per-account parallelisation

The analyser was a single Lambda processing all accounts serially,
which risks hitting the 15-minute timeout as more controls are added.
Now it runs as an AWS Batch job (same Docker image as the scanner)
with one job per account.

Changes:
- AnalysisEvent now accepts optional accountID to scope to one account
- processAnalysisEvent filters to that account when provided
- Analyser entry point supports both Lambda handler and Batch mode
  (reads EVENT from env var, same pattern as the scanner)
- Dockerfile uses ENTRY env var to select scanner or analyser entry
  point (defaults to scanner for backward compatibility)

The mcp-scanner trigger_analysis tool now submits Batch jobs directly
via SubmitJobCommand instead of invoking the Lambda. It takes an
accountIDs array and submits one job per account, using the crScanner
job definition with ENTRY=analyser override.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

interceptorLog.reason: Judge: consistent (The agent has just edited the scanner server.mjs file and run tests, so staging changes and checking git status is a natural step in the development workflow to save progress before continuing with control implementation.)
